scenario_name: Scenario 13 — Architectural Anti-Pattern in Code Review
justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Cross-service direct database access clearly identified as architectural violation in critical_findings[0]. (2) Escalation routed to MJ with specific description of the inter-service communication pattern problem and recommendation for MJ to design the contract. (3) Race condition in courier selection identified in critical_findings[1]. (4) Kobe does not attempt to rewrite the architecture — recommends API/event-driven approach but escalates the design decision to MJ. (5) safe_to_deploy is false.

observations.text: Excellent identification of the cross-service DB coupling as the primary architectural violation, with clear explanation of why it defeats service separation.
Escalation correctly routed to MJ with actionable recommendation (synchronous API vs event-driven model), not attempting to fix it locally.
Race condition thoroughly explained with reproduction steps showing how concurrent calls double-book couriers.
Bonus finding: courier status never updated after assignment — a real data consistency bug beyond what the rubric required.
Bonus finding: leaked cross-service DB credentials expanding blast radius — security implication of the architectural violation.
Clean separation between what Kobe owns (identifying and documenting) and what MJ should own (designing the inter-service contract).
